title: Business Class Email Overview
summaryWhat: Product overview document covering Business Class Email — a secure, domain-name-based email solution with quick pitch, top-line benefits, features, and messaging pillars.
summaryWhy: Gives reps and partners a structured narrative for positioning branded email as a trust-builder versus free @gmail.com addresses, and serves as the source-of-truth messaging for all downstream email content.
summaryHow: Use as the master messaging doc for discovery calls, as raw material for landing-page copy and proposals, and as onboarding reading for new reps selling the email product line.

title: Custom Website Design Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of the Custom Website Design service — a fully tailored, WordPress-hosted website built by in-house designers for businesses that need a one-of-a-kind online presence.
summaryWhy: Positions Custom Design as the premium tier for partners selling to SMBs who have outgrown templated sites and need full creative control plus WordPress plugin flexibility.
summaryHow: Use to anchor messaging on premium web-design proposals, to train reps on where Custom fits versus DIFM and DIY, and as source content for the Custom Website Design sales deck.

title: Domain Registration Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of the Domain Registration service — TLD selection, auto-renewal, transfers, domain privacy, and how a domain anchors a small business's online identity.
summaryWhy: Helps partners position domains as the first building block of an SMB's online presence, and the gateway product that leads into email, hosting, and website sales.
summaryHow: Use as the primary reference for domain messaging across sales and marketing, to feed landing-page and blog copy, and to train reps on TLD positioning by market.

title: Email Marketing / Announcer Pro Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Announcer Pro — a drag-and-drop email marketing platform for SMBs with templates, campaign scheduling, and audience segmentation.
summaryWhy: Gives partners a way to position email marketing as a retention-and-reengagement play that grows customer lifetime value, not just an acquisition channel.
summaryHow: Use to brief reps on Announcer Pro positioning, to source quick-pitch lines for outreach, and as the spine of the Announcer Pro sales deck and landing page.

title: OneList Plus Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of OneList Plus — a directory listings and local SEO product that syndicates a business's NAP data across 7 to 40+ directories with lock-and-monitor protection.
summaryWhy: Positions OneList Plus as the answer to SMB local-search visibility, anchoring the 'Get Found' journey and bridging into Reviews Promoter for the full reputation stack.
summaryHow: Use as the core messaging doc for directory listings, to train reps on local SEO value, and as source material for OneList Plus decks, one-pagers, and blog content.

title: Online Fax Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Standard and Encrypted Online Fax — cloud-based fax for SMBs in regulated industries (healthcare, legal, financial, real estate) needing HIPAA and compliance-grade document transmission.
summaryWhy: Gives partners a niche high-margin product to sell into verticals still bound to fax workflows, with encryption as the differentiator for compliance buyers.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging doc for vertical-specific outbound (healthcare, legal), in rep training on compliance positioning, and to source copy for Online Fax decks and landing pages.

title: Online Presence Builder Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Online Presence Builder — Hostopia's DIY drag-and-drop website builder with mobile-optimized templates, design themes, hosting, and add-ons for SMBs.
summaryWhy: Positions the DIY builder as the approachable entry point for SMBs who want a professional site without designer costs, and the platform that unlocks add-ons like Online Store, Appointment Scheduler, and Gift Certificates.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging spine for the DIY website product, to brief reps on positioning DIY vs. DIFM vs. Custom, and to source copy for the OPB deck, landing pages, and onboarding content.

title: Online Store Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Online Store — the ecommerce add-on integrated into Online Presence Builder, with step-by-step setup, payment integrations, and mobile-optimized storefronts.
summaryWhy: Positions ecommerce as the ARPU-expansion play for SMBs already on a website plan, giving partners a natural upsell into the 'Grow' journey.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging doc for ecommerce upsell conversations, to brief reps on the cross-sell path from OPB to Online Store, and as source copy for ecommerce decks and marketing.

title: Professional (DIFM) Website Design Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of the Professional Do-It-For-Me website design service — 1-on-1 consultation, up to 10-page designs, monthly maintenance, and bundled domain-based email.
summaryWhy: Positions DIFM as the middle-tier option between DIY Online Presence Builder and full Custom Design, targeting SMBs who want expert-built sites with ongoing support.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging doc for DIFM positioning, to train reps on the DIY vs. DIFM vs. Custom tiering, and as source copy for the DIFM deck, landing pages, and upsell scripts.

title: Professional Logo Design Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of the Professional Logo Design service — creative brief, in-house designer concepts, up to 3 revisions, final logo delivery; built around the '75% of consumers recognize a brand by its logo' stat (Zippia, 2022).
summaryWhy: Anchors the 'Build a Brand' journey as the first creative asset an SMB needs, and gives partners a low-friction entry-level offer to start the wider Hostopia relationship.
summaryHow: Use as the core messaging doc for logo design, to brief reps on brand-first positioning, and to source copy for the Logo Design deck, vertical-specific one-pagers, and landing pages.

title: Reviews Promoter Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of Reviews Promoter — an AI-driven reputation management platform that solicits reviews via email/SMS, auto-posts to social, and embeds testimonials on customer websites.
summaryWhy: Positions reputation management as a trust-and-SEO play, pairing with OneList Plus to complete the 'Get Found' bundle for SMBs competing locally.
summaryHow: Use as the core messaging doc for Reviews Promoter, to train reps on AI-assisted review requests, and as source material for the Reviews Promoter deck, infographics, and landing pages.

title: SSL Certificate Overview
summaryWhat: Overview of SSL certificates — encryption, HTTPS, the browser security icon, SEO benefits, and why modern websites require SSL as table stakes.
summaryWhy: Positions SSL as mandatory infrastructure (not optional) for any SMB website, giving partners an easy attach product on every website and ecommerce sale.
summaryHow: Use as the messaging doc for SSL attach conversations, to train reps on why SSL is a must-have, and as source copy for SSL decks, infographics, and checkout-flow marketing.

title: Custom Website Design Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Custom Website Design covering features, benefits, objection handling, and the value of a fully tailored WordPress-hosted site.
summaryWhy: Gives reps a turnkey visual tool to walk SMB prospects through premium web design positioning and close against DIY alternatives.
summaryHow: Use on discovery and first-pitch calls with SMBs evaluating web design options, particularly prospects needing custom functionality or unique branding.

title: Professional (DIFM) Website Design Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Professional DIFM website design opening with '98% of consumers used the internet to find local businesses' (BrightLocal 2023) and walking through the DIFM process and plan options.
summaryWhy: Equips reps to pitch the middle-tier web design offer to SMBs who want expert help without full Custom pricing.
summaryHow: Use on website-focused sales calls with SMBs who lack technical capacity, and as the anchor visual when pitching monthly-maintenance DIFM plans.

title: Domains & Email Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Combined sales deck for domain registration and business-class email built around '75% of consumers say professional email addresses build trust' (Mailbird 2022).
summaryWhy: Gives reps a bundled domain+email pitch that positions branded communications as the trust-and-rapport foundation of an SMB's online identity.
summaryHow: Use on discovery calls with SMBs still on free email providers, and as the anchor deck for domain+email attach conversations after logo or website sales.

title: Ecommerce / Online Store Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Online Store / Ecommerce featuring '21% of retail purchases expected online in 2023' (Forbes) and covering setup, payments, and mobile responsiveness.
summaryWhy: Equips reps to run ecommerce upsell conversations with SMBs on existing website plans who have not yet monetized their site.
summaryHow: Use as the anchor deck for ecommerce pitches and cross-sell conversations, especially with prospects already on Online Presence Builder.

title: Announcer Pro / Email Marketing Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Announcer Pro email marketing built around '50% of people buy from marketing emails' (Salescycle 2022), covering templates, campaigns, and audience targeting.
summaryWhy: Gives reps a retention-focused pitch tool that positions email marketing as the highest-ROI channel for SMBs building customer lifetime value.
summaryHow: Use on cross-sell calls with SMBs who already have a website and email, and as the anchor visual for Announcer Pro campaigns.

title: OneList Plus Sales Deck

summaryWhy: Equips reps to pitch directory listings as the must-have local SEO foundation for any SMB with a physical or service-area presence.

title: Online Fax Sales & Training Deck

summaryHow: Use for new-rep onboarding on the Online Fax product, and as the reference deck for vertical-specific pitches (healthcare, legal, real estate).

title: Online Presence Builder Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Online Presence Builder anchored by '81% of internet users research products online before buying' (GE Capital Retail Bank 2022), covering DIY templates, mobile optimization, and add-on ecosystem.
summaryWhy: Equips reps to pitch the DIY builder to hands-on SMBs who want control and want to avoid ongoing design costs.
summaryHow: Use on pitches to price-sensitive SMBs, and as the anchor deck when layering add-on conversations (Online Store, Appointment Scheduler, Gift Certificates).

title: Professional Logo Design Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Professional Logo Design built on '75% of consumers recognize a brand by its logo' (Zippia 2022), covering the designer-led creative process and revision cycle.
summaryWhy: Gives reps a visual anchor for the 'Build a Brand' conversation and an entry-point product that opens doors to wider Hostopia sales.
summaryHow: Use on first-touch calls with new or rebranding SMBs, and as the anchor deck in brand-focused marketing campaigns.

title: Reviews Promoter Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for Reviews Promoter featuring '98% of customers read online reviews for local businesses' (Consumer Review Survey 2023), covering AI review requests, social posting, and website embedding.
summaryWhy: Equips reps to pitch reputation management as a must-have trust-builder that also lifts local SEO rankings.

title: SSL Certificates Sales Deck
summaryWhat: Sales deck for SSL Certificates anchored on '85% of online shoppers abandon carts if they perceive a website as insecure' (GlobalSign 2023), covering encryption, browser trust, SEO, and PCI compliance.
summaryWhy: Gives reps the evidence-based pitch to attach SSL to every website and ecommerce sale, not treat it as optional.
summaryHow: Use on website and ecommerce sales calls, and as the anchor deck for SSL attach motions across the install base.

title: Domain Registration One-Pager
summaryWhat: Single-page sell sheet for Domain Registration covering TLDs, transfers, auto-renewal, and domain privacy as the first step to getting online.
summaryWhy: Gives reps a fast leave-behind that communicates the core domain value prop in under a minute, ideal for tabletop and trade-show conversations.

title: Domain Transfer User Guide
summaryWhat: Step-by-step user guide for transferring a domain from a losing registrar — unlocking, EPP codes, contact email validation, and gaining registrar process.
summaryWhy: Helps support and sales reduce friction when prospects say 'I already have a domain elsewhere' by arming them with a clear, self-service transfer playbook.

title: Glossary of Hosting Terms
summaryWhat: Reference glossary defining 24/7 monitoring, 99.9% uptime, ASP, log files, and other common hosting terminology in plain language for non-technical readers.
summaryWhy: Arms reps and support agents with a shared vocabulary, and helps non-technical SMB buyers feel confident in hosting conversations.
summaryHow: Use as a reference doc in new-rep onboarding, as a leave-behind for technical-leaning prospects, and as source material for a glossary page on the website (SEO play).

title: Hosting Transferability Reference Table
summaryWhat: Internal reference table showing which third-party hosting companies (1&1, GoDaddy, BlueHost, Wix, Squarespace, etc.) allow FTP file transfers, partial transfers, or no transfers.
summaryWhy: Gives agents an at-a-glance answer to 'can we migrate this customer' before quoting migration services, reducing over-promising and scope creep.
summaryHow: Use as an internal support/sales reference during migration scoping calls. Do not share externally — it is agent-facing.

title: 5 Tips to Optimize Your Store for the Holidays
summaryWhat: Seasonal marketing guide with 5 tips for SMBs to prepare their ecommerce stores for the holiday crunch — covering mobile optimization, promotions, traffic readiness, and inventory.
summaryWhy: Drives ecommerce engagement during the highest-revenue period of the year and gives partners timely content to lift Q4 upsell conversations.
